title: OCI Runtime Specification
description: Schema for the OCI (Open Container Initiative) runtime specification config.json used by containerd to define container configuration including process, mounts, Linux-specific settings, and resource constraints.

ociVersion.description: Version of the OCI runtime specification this config conforms to

mounts.description: List of additional filesystem mounts beyond the root filesystem

hostname.description: Hostname to set inside the container

domainname.description: Domain name to set inside the container

annotations.description: Arbitrary metadata key-value pairs associated with the container using reverse domain name notation for keys

Root.description: Root filesystem configuration for the container

path.description: Path to the root filesystem bundle, either absolute or relative to the bundle directory

readonly.description: Mount the root filesystem as read-only inside the container

Mount.description: A filesystem mount point inside the container

destination.description: Absolute path inside the container where the filesystem will be mounted

source.description: Source path on the host or device name for the mount

type.description: Filesystem type such as proc, tmpfs, sysfs, devpts, or bind

options.description: Mount options such as nosuid, noexec, nodev, ro, rw, rbind

Process.description: Container process configuration defining what runs inside the container

terminal.description: Attach a pseudo-terminal to the process

consoleSize.description: Size of the console terminal in characters

cwd.description: Working directory inside the container for the process

env.description: Environment variables for the process in KEY=VALUE format

args.description: Command and arguments to execute as the container entrypoint

commandLine.description: Full command line string for Windows containers

rlimits.description: Resource limits applied to the container process

noNewPrivileges.description: Prevent the process from gaining additional privileges via setuid or capabilities

apparmorProfile.description: Name of the AppArmor profile to apply to the process

oomScoreAdj.description: Adjustment to the OOM killer score for this process

selinuxLabel.description: SELinux label to apply to the process

User.description: User identity under which the container process runs

uid.description: User ID for the container process

gid.description: Primary group ID for the container process

umask.description: File creation mask for the container process

additionalGids.description: Supplementary group IDs for the container process

Capabilities.description: Linux capabilities sets controlling fine-grained privilege for the process

bounding.description: Bounding set that limits which capabilities can be acquired

effective.description: Capabilities currently active for the process

inheritable.description: Capabilities preserved across an execve call

permitted.description: Maximum set of capabilities the process may use

ambient.description: Capabilities applied to non-privileged programs on execve

Rlimit.description: A POSIX resource limit for the container process

type.description: Resource limit type such as RLIMIT_NOFILE or RLIMIT_NPROC

hard.description: Hard ceiling for the resource limit

soft.description: Enforced limit that can be raised up to the hard limit

Hooks.description: Lifecycle hooks for container creation, startup, and teardown

prestart.description: Hooks called after the container is created but before it starts (deprecated in favor of createRuntime)

createRuntime.description: Hooks called during container creation after the runtime environment is set up

createContainer.description: Hooks called during container creation after the container namespace is entered

startContainer.description: Hooks called before the user-specified process is executed

poststart.description: Hooks called after the user-specified process has started

poststop.description: Hooks called after the container process has exited

Hook.description: A lifecycle hook specifying a command to execute at a particular container lifecycle event

path.description: Absolute path to the hook executable

args.description: Arguments to pass to the hook executable including argv[0]

env.description: Environment variables for the hook in KEY=VALUE format

timeout.description: Maximum time in seconds to wait for the hook to complete

Linux.description: Linux-specific container configuration including namespaces, cgroups, seccomp, and device access

uidMappings.description: User ID mappings for user namespaces

gidMappings.description: Group ID mappings for user namespaces

devices.description: Devices to make available inside the container

cgroupsPath.description: Path to the cgroup for the container, either absolute or relative to the cgroup mount

rootfsPropagation.description: Mount propagation mode for the root filesystem

maskedPaths.description: Paths inside the container that should be masked (hidden) from the process

readonlyPaths.description: Paths inside the container that should be mounted read-only

sysctl.description: Kernel parameters to set inside the container namespace

mountLabel.description: SELinux mount label for the container filesystem

Namespace.description: A Linux namespace used for container isolation

path.description: Path to an existing namespace to join instead of creating a new one

IDMapping.description: User or group ID mapping between host and container for user namespaces

Device.description: A device node to create inside the container

type.description: Device type: c (character), b (block), u (unbuffered), or p (FIFO)

path.description: Absolute path for the device node inside the container

fileMode.description: File permission mode for the device node

uid.description: User ID of the device node owner

gid.description: Group ID of the device node owner

Resources.description: Linux cgroup resource constraints for the container

swap.description: Total memory plus swap limit in bytes, -1 for unlimited

kernel.description: Kernel memory limit in bytes (deprecated in cgroup v2)

disableOOMKiller.description: Disable the OOM killer for this container

cpu.description: CPU resource limits and scheduling configuration

shares.description: Relative CPU share weight for scheduling

quota.description: CPU time quota per period in microseconds, -1 for no limit

cpus.description: CPUs to use expressed as a list or range (e.g. 0-3, 0,1)

mems.description: Memory nodes to use expressed as a list or range

pids.description: Process count limits for the container

limit.description: Maximum number of processes in the container, -1 for unlimited

weight.description: Default block I/O weight from 10 to 1000
Block I/O weight for this device

Seccomp.description: Seccomp (secure computing mode) configuration for syscall filtering

defaultAction.description: Default action when a syscall does not match any rule

architectures.description: Architectures to apply the seccomp filter to

syscalls.description: Syscall-specific rules for the seccomp filter

names.description: Syscall names this rule applies to

action.description: Action to take when the syscall matches

errnoRet.description: Errno return value when action is SCMP_ACT_ERRNO

args.description: Argument-level conditions for matching this rule

value.description: Value to compare the argument against
